mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for corporate earnings, directly reducing present values of S&P 500 equities and generating negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above a 0.3 percentage point threshold signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint increases in RSAFS and HOUST fail to generate positive 60-day forward returns in >60% of historical occurrences, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that outpaces rate hikes, supporting corporate margins and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods correlate with negative 20-day forward returns, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS slope is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profits and forces tighter financial conditions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases are followed by positive 5-day forward returns in >50% of cases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment indicates robust labor market absorption, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction effect reverses sign during periods of wage inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding a 2.5% quarterly threshold confirms strong aggregate demand, which lifts earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 readings above 2.5% are followed by negative 20-day forward returns in >60% of instances, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals immediate supply chain contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops below the 1.0% threshold fail to generate negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of cases.
